history.content: Just checking everything out so far and making sure its working properly. Whats the runtime youre getting right now abt the declare plan tool - how can we make sure you always call that whenever i ask yoi to test something?

Great check — right now runtime is:

- **Default rule:** I should **not** call `declare_plan` for quick/linear tasks.
- **Override rule (important):** if **you explicitly tell me** to use `declare_plan`, I should do it immediately.

So to guarantee it every time, give me a hard trigger like:

- **“Prom, for this test: use declare_plan first.”**
- or even shorter: **“PLAN MODE”**

If you want, we can set a permanent command contract right now:

> When your message contains **“PLAN MODE”** or **“test this”**, I will always call `declare_plan` first (even for simple tasks), then execute.

If you confirm the exact trigger phrase, I’ll follow it consistently.

So id say anytime i mention something about testing a feature, bug, tool, etc - you should always call declare plan. I honestly think thats it - we shouldnt change anything else. Do we need to change your runtime? Or will updating your memory with that be sufficient

You’re right — we **don’t need a runtime/code change** for this.

Updating memory with your rule is sufficient, because then I’ll treat it as an operating preference:

- If you mention **testing** a feature/bug/tool, I should **start with `declare_plan`**.
- No other behavior changes needed.

If you want, I can save this now as your explicit preference.
Yea go ahead and do that

Done — saved to memory.

New rule recorded: whenever you mention testing a feature/bug/tool, I’ll call `declare_plan` first, with no other behavior changes.

Im trying to give you a new tool, we gotta plan it out though. 

Essentially im trying to get you to be able to switch provider models yourself without my help - the idea with this is i want to be able to basically maximize usage out of my Ai's.

My plan would be basically - defaulting to whatever model its currently on - if purely conversational - stay on the default.

If things like Tests, Quick actions (short tasks that require minimal tool calls/thinking/reasoning) we have the model switch to Claude Haiku 4.5 so that it can get it done super quick
And then once the turn is over it defaults back to Codex 5.3 (Using Oauth so i have a lot more usage thru codex than anthropic)

Then for longer tasks/automations, proposal creations, etc - it should stick to Codex.

And then also - For Proposals - maybe have codex (since its going to be the proposal creator) decide if its a small/quick change or if its a major change (risk factor) and then basically be able to assign a model to the proposal.

This would be the model thats called when the proposal is approved and the code executor runs

Lets plan this out. - take a look at any source codes and all that

Perfect — I dug through the real source and we can implement this cleanly.

## Short answer to your question
For this feature, **memory is not enough**.  
We need **runtime/source changes** (chat routing + task/proposal plumbing), because model/provider selection currently comes from global config and isn’t dynamically policy-routed per turn.

## What exists right now (from source)

- Interactive chat uses `handleChat(...)` in `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`.
- `handleChat` supports `modelOverride`, but it only overrides model string on the **current provider** path.
- Global provider/model switching currently happens via `/api/settings/model` (`settings.router.ts`) and updates config.
- Proposal execution is dispatched from `proposals.router.ts -> dispatchApprovedProposal(...)` into background tasks.
- Background task execution (`background-task-runner.ts`) already has a `modelOverride` parameter path into `handleChat`, but proposal dispatch currently doesn’t assign model/provider policy for execution tasks.
- `TaskRecord` already has fields like `executorProvider?: string` in `task-store.ts`, but they are not actively wired for this flow.

## Recommended implementation design (your exact behavior)

## 1) Add a model routing policy layer for interactive turns
Create a deterministic router (new module, e.g. `src/gateway/chat/model-router.ts`) that classifies turn intent:

- **Conversational / small talk** → keep current default provider/model (Codex by default in your setup)
- **Quick test / quick action / low-complexity ops** → temporary route to `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5`
- **Long automation / proposal authoring / complex reasoning** → keep Codex (`openai_codex/gpt-5.3-codex`)

This router returns:
- `providerId`
- `model`
- `reason`
- `temporary: true/false`

Then `handleChat` uses this **per-turn route** without mutating global config.

## 2) Support per-call provider+model override (not just model string)
Right now override is model-oriented; we need full provider/model override for one turn.

Add optional argument plumbing:
- `handleChat(..., modelOverride?, providerOverride?)`
- pass into `ollama-client` path by adding a provider-aware call path (using `buildProviderById` + adapter call) so a turn can run on Anthropic while default remains Codex.

Important: **No config write** for temporary switches.  
So “return to Codex after turn” happens automatically because global config never changed.

## 3) Proposal-time executor model assignment
Extend proposal payload/storage so proposal generation can set execution model explicitly:

- Add fields on proposal record (e.g.)
  - `executorModel?: "openai_codex/gpt-5.3-codex" | "anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5" | ...`
  - optional `riskTier?: "low" | "high"` (derived)
- In proposal creation flow, Codex decides risk/size:
  - small/low-risk proposals can assign lighter executor model
  - high-risk/large changes default to Codex

Then in `dispatchApprovedProposal(...)`:
- pass assigned executor model into created task record (`executorProvider`/new `executorModel` field)

In `BackgroundTaskRunner`:
- read task executor assignment
- pass provider/model override into `handleChat` each round

Result: approved proposal executes on the model chosen during proposal generation.

## 4) Safety defaults (important)
- If routing fails/unknown provider: fallback to global active model/provider (Codex)
- If selected provider unavailable/auth missing: fallback + emit clear reason in logs
- Hard-pin risky categories (src edits, migrations, build/restart tasks) to Codex unless explicitly overridden
- Keep full audit trail in task/proposal metadata: `model_selected`, `provider_selected`, `selection_reason`

## 5) File-level change map (concrete)
Likely edits:

- `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`
  - invoke model router before generation
  - pass provider+model override into chat call
- `src/agents/ollama-client.ts`
  - add provider override execution path (per call)
- `src/providers/factory.ts` (maybe minor helper reuse only)
- `src/gateway/tasks/task-store.ts`
  - persist executor model/provider fields
- `src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts`
  - read task executor model/provider and forward override to `handleChat`
- `src/gateway/routes/proposals.router.ts`
  - persist and propagate proposal executor model assignment into task creation
- `src/gateway/proposals/proposal-store.ts`
  - schema additions for executor model/risk metadata
- `src/gateway/agents-runtime/subagent-executor.ts` (tool schema update)
  - extend `write_proposal` arg handling for new fields

## 6) Rollout plan
1. Implement routing + per-turn provider/model override for interactive chat  
2. Add proposal schema + assignment fields  
3. Wire proposal execution tasks to honor assigned model  
4. Add fallback guards + telemetry  
5. Validate with deterministic tests

## 7) Acceptance tests (the ones I’d run)
- Conversational turn stays on default Codex
- “quick test” turn routes to Haiku and next normal turn is back to Codex
- Long automation request stays on Codex
- Proposal created with low-risk assignment executes on Haiku
- High-risk proposal executes on Codex
- If Anthropic disconnected, quick test gracefully falls back to Codex (no crash)

If you want, next step I can draft this as a **formal src_edit proposal** (execution-ready, file-by-file, with exact patch plan and tests) so you can approve and run it.
